_meta.purpose: Few-shot examples for the Bettability Classifier LLM prompt. Include these as in-context examples before asking the LLM to score a new topic. One strong positive (10/10) and one strong negative (4/10 with double veto) — this pair calibrates both dimensions: recognizing bettability AND recognizing the 'hot but unbettable' trap.
_meta.usage: Prepend to system prompt. Keep examples stable across calls so scoring is reproducible.

examples.label: POSITIVE_EXAMPLE - perfect bettability
examples.topic_description: Weekly pump price adjustment for diesel and gasoline in the Philippines. DOE announces every Monday; oil companies implement every Tuesday.

R.reason: DOE publishes exact peso/liter figures; cleanly verifiable

S.reason: DOE is the sole authoritative source under Philippine Oil Deregulation Law; cross-verified by oil companies
S.proposed_source: DOE Weekly Oil Price Monitoring Bulletin

T.reason: Weekly cycle; every Tuesday implementation is a natural anchor

U.reason: Both direction and magnitude vary weekly based on crude futures + FX; no dominant prior

H.reason: Multi-source news coverage + Reddit engagement + FB disinformation activity (fact-checkers intervening) = broad public attention

scoring.suggested_market_question: Will Philippine pump diesel prices see a NET INCREASE in the week of {week_start}?

examples.teaching_point: When R/S/T/U are all strong AND the topic is hot, disposition is TOP. Extra signal: recurring cadence (weekly) means one topic spawns perpetual markets.

examples.label: NEGATIVE_EXAMPLE - hot but unbettable, double veto
examples.topic_description: Viral rumor that a famous Philippine showbiz couple (KathNiel-type national CP) has broken up. Multiple anonymous-source reports; managed silence from parties.

R.reason: 'Officially confirm breakup' is nominally binary but in Philippine showbiz culture, couples rarely make formal announcements. Definition is fuzzy.

S.reason: VETO. No authoritative resolution source exists. Celebrity Instagram posts can be deleted; management statements are deliberately ambiguous; media outlets contradict each other citing 'sources close to'.

T.reason: 'By end of Q2' is an artificial time box, not a natural event deadline. High risk of expiring with neither confirmation nor denial.

U.reason: VETO. Outcome is effectively predetermined. When 100+ articles already treat the breakup as fact, the probability of formal confirmation within any given window is < 15% (standard PH showbiz playbook is silence). Market will crush to one side, zero liquidity on the other.

H.reason: Extreme heat: bilingual coverage, 14+ distinct outlets, viral Reddit threads, fact-checkers intervening on FB misinformation

scoring.rejection_explanation: Double veto on S (no authoritative resolution source) and U (outcome effectively predetermined by the time it's this hot). Heat alone is insufficient — it may even be a red flag that the story has already resolved.

examples.teaching_point: High heat with S=0 or U=0 is the classic trap. The rule: when H=2 but S or U is 0, the topic goes to DROP_BUT_DIGEST (shown in non-market 'hot topics' sidebar, never listed as a market). This protects market quality while still using the attention signal for product engagement.

disposition_mapping.WATCH: 5 <= total <= 6 OR (total >= 7 AND veto_dimensions has exactly one of ['T'])

hard_rules: Any topic with S=0 CANNOT be disposition=TOP regardless of total score
Any topic with U=0 CANNOT be disposition=TOP regardless of total score
Topics with T=0 can reach CANDIDATE but not TOP (pending a known resolution date)
Topics with R=0 must be rewritten by LLM into a well-defined question and re-scored, not directly dropped
